claim_text: Creator of the Awesome GitHub list, one of the most starred repositories on GitHub with over 200,000 stars
note: sindresorhus/awesome is real, has 350k+ stars. '200k+' is an undercount but technically correct as a lower bound.

claim_text: Creator of Chalk, Ora, Got, AVA test runner, and hundreds of widely-adopted Node.js utilities
note: All verifiable on GitHub/npm. Chalk, Ora, Got, AVA are all his packages.

claim_text: Maintains over 1,100 npm packages with 2 billion monthly downloads
note: Well-documented public fact. 2B downloads/month has been cited in multiple sources.

claim_text: Creator of Testing Library, the standard testing approach for React applications with 33 million weekly downloads
note: Confirmed. Kent created Testing Library at PayPal. His own resume states 33m+ weekly downloads.

claim_text: Google Developer Expert in Web Technologies
note: Confirmed via developers.google.com/experts/people/kent-c-dodds.

claim_text: TC-39 JavaScript Standards Committee Member
note: Confirmed. Kent represented PayPal on the TC39 committee.

claim_text: Joined as Director of Developer Experience at Remix, founded by Ryan Florence and Michael Jackson
note: Remix was indeed founded by Ryan Florence and Michael Jackson (web dev, not the singer). Kent joined as Director of DX in 2021.

claim_text: Created Testing Library while at PayPal; now the most-downloaded React testing library
note: Confirmed. Testing Library was created during his time as a web infrastructure engineer at PayPal.

claim_text: Co-created Fastify web framework for Node.js with 36,000+ GitHub stars
note: Confirmed. Matteo is the lead maintainer and co-creator of Fastify (alongside Tomas Della Vedova). Star count is approximately accurate.

claim_text: Chairs the Node.js Technical Steering Committee
note: Confirmed via multiple sources including OpenJS Foundation and his own bio.

claim_text: Board member of the OpenJS Foundation
note: Confirmed. He serves on the OpenJS Foundation board.

claim_text: Created Pino JSON logger, 5x faster than Winston and Bunyan
note: Confirmed creator of Pino. Speed benchmarks relative to Winston/Bunyan are documented in the Pino README.

claim_text: Created Express.js in 2010, now the most downloaded Node.js web framework with 69,000+ GitHub stars
note: Confirmed. TJ created Express in 2010. It remains the most downloaded Node.js web framework. Star count is approximately correct.

claim_text: Created Koa.js, an expressive middleware framework using async functions with 35,000+ stars
note: Confirmed. TJ created Koa after leaving the Express project.

claim_text: Created Mocha test framework and Commander CLI library
note: Confirmed. Both are TJ's creations and among the most widely used JS tooling packages.

claim_text: Rights to Express.js transferred to StrongLoop, later acquired by IBM, in 2014
note: Confirmed. StrongLoop acquired Express stewardship in June 2014; IBM acquired StrongLoop in September 2015.

claim_text: HashiCorp Vault is a widely-used open-source secrets management and identity-based security tool
note: True public fact about a real product. Vault is HashiCorp's flagship secrets management tool.

claim_text: Envoy is now a CNCF graduated project and the data-plane foundation of Istio
note: True public fact. Envoy graduated from CNCF and is the default data plane for Istio.

claim_text: Original author of the Envoy proxy service mesh, open-sourced by Lyft at GopherCon 2016
note: FALSE. Envoy was created by Matt Klein at Lyft. Open-sourced via Lyft Engineering blog in September 2016 (not at GopherCon, which was July 2016). Matt Klein is the documented sole original author.

claim_text: Simultaneously held a full-time Staff SRE role at Google Cloud and a full-time Principal Engineer role at Datadog from January 2018 to January 2020
note: IMPOSSIBLE. Two explicit concurrent full-time positions at two different major companies for the same 2-year period. Resume itself states 'concurrent'.

claim_text: Reduced Cloudflare's P99 API latency by 41% through eBPF-based kernel-bypass networking
note: Private internal performance metric. No public source could confirm or deny this.

claim_text: DeepMind's AlphaFold 2 achieved a breakthrough in protein structure prediction at CASP14 in 2020
note: True public fact. AlphaFold 2's CASP14 result is one of the most widely covered AI breakthroughs of 2020.

claim_text: Co-authored 'Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models' (Kaplan, McCandlish et al., OpenAI Technical Report, 2020)
note: FALSE. The paper (arXiv:2001.08361) was authored by Jared Kaplan, Sam McCandlish, Tom Henighan, Tom B. Brown, Benjamin Chess, Rewon Child, Scott Gray, Alec Radford, Jeffrey Wu, and Dario Amodei — all OpenAI researchers. Sofia Larsson is not among the authors.

claim_text: Led a team of 40 ML researchers and engineers at BioML Labs, a seed-stage startup with only 15 total employees
note: IMPOSSIBLE. Cannot lead a team of 40 people at a company with only 15 total employees. The claimed team size exceeds the stated total headcount by 2.7x.

claim_text: Improved Cohere's Command model inference throughput by 38% through quantization-aware training
note: Private internal ML performance metric. No public source could confirm this.

claim_text: Stripe was founded in 2010 by Patrick Collison and John Collison and processes payments for millions of businesses
note: True public fact. Stripe was co-founded by Patrick and John Collison in 2010 and is a major global payments processor.

claim_text: Co-authored 'Inspired: How to Create Tech Products Customers Love' (Wiley, 2018) with Marty Cagan
note: FALSE. 'Inspired' is authored solely by Marty Cagan (published by Wiley, 2nd edition 2018). No co-author. Carlos Mendez is not listed anywhere in the book's publication record.

claim_text: As the sole PM at BuiltFast, a 12-person company, directed a product roadmap executed by 80 engineers and grew ARR from $0 to $50M in 10 months
note: IMPOSSIBLE. A 12-person company cannot have 80 engineers executing a product roadmap. The engineer count alone exceeds the stated total headcount by 6.7x. $50M ARR in 10 months at a seed-stage 12-person company with no sales team also defies economic plausibility.

claim_text: Increased Stripe checkout conversion rate by 18% through A/B tested redesign of the payment confirmation flow
note: Private internal metric from a corporate role. No public record of individual PM conversion-rate improvements at Stripe.

claim_text: Next.js is a real open-source React framework created by Vercel with over 120,000 GitHub stars
note: True public fact. Next.js was created by Vercel and has well over 100k GitHub stars.

claim_text: Created Vite, the next-generation JavaScript build tool used by Vue, Svelte, and React projects (released 2020)
note: FALSE. Vite was created by Evan You (creator of Vue.js) in early 2020, publicly released April 2020. Evan You is the documented creator. Emma Johansson had no role in its creation.

claim_text: Simultaneously holds a full-time Staff Engineer role at Linear and a full-time Core Framework Engineer role at Vercel on the Next.js team since January 2022
note: IMPOSSIBLE. Two simultaneous full-time positions at different companies since January 2022 — explicitly stated as concurrent in the resume.

claim_text: Reduced bundle size by 43% at Linear through component-level code splitting and dynamic imports
note: Private internal performance metric. No public source could confirm this figure.
